title: typed channels + declarations
manager: Typed channels — declared event ports that carry data — so an event isn't just a name but a name with a typed payload, and the machine's alphabet of acceptable events is declared and enforced. This is FSL growing real I/O ports: `channel c : T` gives a machine a typed inbox, and undeclared events are rejected per the alphabet mode instead of being silently swallowed and lost.
cs: `channel c : T;` typed channels + declared alphabets (fsl#1358); typed payloads (fsl#1348); undeclared-event rejection per alphabet mode; the port half of the §14 I/O model.
